what_it_does: Defines an HTML page for The Wayfinder, creates a mount node, loads shared styles and the wayfinder runtime, mounts a registry-driven Wayfinder view from /registry/wayfinder.json, and mounts a draggable Vee dock module.
observations: The page title names The Wayfinder as the substrate's address map.
It loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts.
It applies shared piece and phone styles, then overrides body layout so the page can scroll from the top.
The Wayfinder view is mounted into #mount using DNA.mountWayfinder with /registry/wayfinder.json as its data source.
A module script imports and mounts the Vee dock and injects palette CSS variables.
standouts: Registry-driven Wayfinder mounting is the page's central behavior.
The body layout override explicitly adapts a shared centered piece style to a tall scrolling index.
The Vee dock is embedded directly in the page shell.
conventions: Uses a minimal HTML shell with runtime-driven rendering.
Uses absolute project asset paths for styles, runtime scripts, data, and modules.
Keeps page-specific layout adjustment inline in the document head.
Uses comments to label conceptual layers such as the instrument and the V.
concerns: Depends on the global DNA object from /surface/runtime/wayfinder-view.js without a local fallback.
Uses absolute asset paths, so the page assumes a specific hosting root.
Loads remote fonts from Google Fonts.
notes: This file is the HTML integration shell for a live registry-backed Wayfinder page plus the embedded Vee dock.
questions: What event consumer handles the documented wayfinder:select interaction?
Should the registry URL be configurable outside this file?
Is the body layout override intended only for this page or for all tall index pages?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file's behavior: it presents a Wayfinder address map rendered from a registry and adds the Vee dock.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make the Wayfinder a reusable, live, registry-backed index page while preserving the project's visual surface and persistent Vee interface.
novelty: The file frames a simple HTML runtime mount as an instrument and combines a registry-rendered address map with an always-present draggable dock.
contribution: Provides the HTML entry point that wires a registry-backed Wayfinder view, top-flowing page layout, shared surface styling, and embedded dock behavior into one page.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML shell for app/wayfinder.html that loads shared surface assets, renders a Wayfinder index from /registry/wayfinder.json, and mounts the Vee dock.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for presenting the project's address map as a live, reusable, data-driven Wayfinder page with the persistent V interface attached.
